chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P.No.D-844 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Order on contempt application. M.A.No.9140 of 2022. 26.11.2025 Petitioner present in person. = The captioned petition was disposed of on 02.06.2021 with directions to respondents to handover the up-to-date ‘service book’ to petitioner and to release his salary within seven days from the date of order. Where after, the listed application for contempt proceeding was filed by the petitioner with allegation that compliance has not yet been made. This Court vide order dated 21.12.2021 observed that Assistant Director Pakistan Bait-ul-Mal had put his appearance before this Court and submitted reply to the contempt application, whereby he stated that petitioner is/was drawing his due salary; and, in compliance of the order, up-to-date ‘service book’ was handed over to him, vide office order dated 14.06.2021, which was duly acknowledged by the petitioner. He also submitted copies of pay slip of the petitioner along with copy of office order dated 14.06.2021 containing acknowledgment receipt of ‘service book’ by the petitioner. On being confronted with this position of the case, petitioner in his abortive attempt submitted that he was / is drawing less salary than the similar placed employees. However, contempt application was disposed of. He further submitted that personal identity number was/is missing in the said documents and deduction of 12 basic in sum of Rs.2170/- was also shown in the pay-slip which was not available and as such compliance in the earlier order dated 27.5.2021 and the disposal order has made in the matter dated 02.6.2021 were being violated in its letter and spirit therefore, respectively time was sought by the respondents but today they are called absent without intimation; however, the stance of the petitioner cannot be entertained at this stage after disposal of the matter even in this contempt application has already been disposed of; therefore, no further indulgence is required.

However, contempt application was disposed of. He further submitted that personal identity number was/is missing in the said documents and deduction of 12 basic in sum of Rs.2170/- was also shown in the pay-slip which was not available and as such compliance in the earlier order dated 27.5.2021 and the disposal order has made in the matter dated 02.6.2021 were being violated in its letter and spirit therefore, respectively time was sought by the respondents but today they are called absent without intimation; however, the stance of the petitioner cannot be entertained at this stage after disposal of the matter even in this contempt application has already been disposed of; therefore, no further indulgence is required. If, his cause is still subsist he may file fresh petition. This contempt application stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa,
